title: Fuel cell architecture
abstract: It is an object of the invention is a fuel cell system architecture provides a good weight distribution, improved trunk space without reducing the security against front-end and rear end collision. The above objective is accomplished by a fuel cell vehicle comprising: - A rear vessel for hydrogen gas located in the rear part of the vehicle, - A front vessel for hydrogen gas located in the front part of the vehicle, - A hydrogen dosing unit, - A hydrogen control unit connected to the hydrogen dosing unit, to the rear vessel and to the front vessel, the hydrogen control unit being provided with first means for equalizing the pressure between the first rear vessel and the front vessel, the hydrogen control unit being provided with second means for transferring hydrogen at a predetermined pressure level to the hydrogen dosing unit from the two vessels, - An air supply, - A fuel cell connected to the hydrogen dosing unit and the air supply, the air supply being provided with means for providing the fuel cell with air, the hydrogen dosing unit providing the fuel cell with hydrogen, - A battery, - A DC/DC- converter, - The fuel cell being connected to the battery so as to provide the battery with energy, the battery and the DC/DC-converter being interconnected so as to exchange energy, the fuel cell and the battery, being located at the bottom of the vehicle.
claims: Claims Vehicle with a fuel cell system comprising: - a rear vessel (V1 ) for hydrogen gas located in the rear part of the vehicle, - a hydrogen dosing unit (162), - an air supply (121 ); - a fuel cell (120) connected to the hydrogen dosing unit (1 62) and the air supply (121 ), the air supply being provided with means for providing the fuel cell with air, the hydrogen dosing unit (162) providing the fuel cell (120) with hydrogen, - a battery (144), - a DC/DC- converter (141 ), characterised in that the vehicle further comprises: - a front vessel (V2) for hydrogen gas located in the front part of the vehicle, - a hydrogen control unit (1 61 ) connected to the hydrogen dosing unit (1 62), to the rear vessel (V1 ) and to the front vessel (V2), the hydrogen control unit (1 61 ) being provided with first means for equalizing the pressure between the first rear vessel (V1 ) and the front vessel, the hydrogen control unit (1 61 ) being provided with second means for transferring hydrogen at a predetermined pressure level to the hydrogen dosing unit (1 62) from the two vessels (V1 ; V2), and - the fuel cell (120) being connected to the battery (144) so as to provide the battery with energy, the battery and the DC/DC-converter being interconnected so as to exchange energy, the fuel cell (120) and the battery (144), being located at the bottom of the vehicle. Vehicle according to the preceding claim, the fuel cell (120) being connected to the DC/DC-converter (141 ) so at to provide the DC/DC converter with energy. Vehicle according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the DC/DC-converter (141 ) is located at the bottom of the vehicle. 4. Vehicle according any of the preceding claims, comprising a super capacitor (146), the fuel cell (120) being connected to the super capacitor (146) so as to provide the super capacitor (146) with energy, the super capacitor (146) being interconnected to the battery (144) so as to exchange energy, and the super capacitor (146) being interconnected to the DC/DC- converter (141 ) so as to exchange energy. 5. Vehicle according to the preceding claim, the super capacitor (146) being located at the bottom of the vehicle. 6. Vehicle according to the preceding claim, wherein the super capacitor (146) is adapted to be integrated in the bottom frame of the vehicle. 7. Vehicle according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the weight difference between the two vessels (V1 , V2) is less than 30%, preferably less than 15%, most preferably less than 1 %. 8. Vehicle according to any one the preceding claims, wherein the front vessel (V2) is located above the feet of the driver and the passenger in the front seat, in the cavity under the dashboard. 9. Vehicle according to claim 8, wherein the air conducts are located on the side of the vehicle. 10. Vehicle according to claim 8, wherein the air conducts are located along or under the floor of the vehicle.
